title: Doric- China Βarometer part 2/2
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas and Helen Vlassi

CRUDE OIL/NATURAL GAS
China brought in 40.24 million tonnes of crude oil in September, equivalent to 9.79 million barrels per day, or circa 2.0 percent down from a year earlier. Decreasing by 0.3 percent from a month earlier, September's reading altered the upward trend of the last two months, as independent refiners curbed throughput amid thin margins and lacklustre demand. From January to September, crude imports totalled 370.41 million tonnes, down circa 4.3 percent from a year earlier.

children.title: Main
children.summary: By Michalis Voutsinas and Helen Vlassi

children.title: CRUDE OIL/NATURAL GAS
children.summary: China brought in 40.24 million tonnes of crude oil in September, equivalent to 9.79 million barrels per day, or circa 2.0 percent down from a year earlier. Decreasing by 0.3 percent from a month earlier, September's reading altered the upward trend of the last two months, as independent refiners curbed throughput amid thin margins and lacklustre demand.

children.title: IRON ORE
children.summary: China brought in 99.71 million tonnes of iron ore in September, up by circa 4.3 percent year-on-year. During the first nine months of 2022, China imported 822.54 million tonnes of iron ore, a dive of 2.3 percent from 841.56 million tonnes reported in the same period a year earlier.

children.title: COAL
children.summary: China imported 33.05 million tonnes of coal in September, up 0.5 percent yearon-year. On a monthly basis, China's coal imports stood 12.2 percent higher.

children.title: GRAINS
children.summary: China's soybean imports in September rose by 12.0 percent from a year earlier to 7.72 million tonnes. China's September imports stood 7.7 percent higher than a reading of 7.17 million tonnes in the previous month, reversing last five-month trend of low arrivals.

children.title: MINORS (EXPORTS)
children.summary: China fertilizer exports in September increased to 3.14 million tonnes, yet still remaining 5.7 percent lower year-onyear. During the first nine months of 2022, China exported circa 16.9 million tonnes of fertilizers, down by circa 40.3 percent year-on-year.

children.title: MINORS (IMPORTS)
children.summary: China's steel imports during September fell by 29.3 percent year-on-year to 0.9 million tonnes. From January to September, Chinese steel imports stood 22.1 percent down from the respective period a year earlier, or at 8.34 million tonnes.
